conflicts.reason: Most frequent hex across 12+ source files; sub-pixel variance within ΔE<2

_meta.visual_identity_notes: HOSQ uses an art-forward, psychedelic visual language. Brand backgrounds feature abstract organic shapes rendered with heavy grain/noise textures, layered transparencies, and saturated color fields. The visual system blends experimental art aesthetics with clean Swiss-style typography grids. Content slides use a warm cream surface (#EBE2D6) with black text, while promotional materials employ full-bleed vibrant gradient backgrounds. The logo (diamond prism + 'hosq' wordmark) always appears in solid black or light beige — never colorized. SMM templates reveal a complete modular post system with standardized layout grids across multiple content types (lectures, partners, bios, quotes, portraits, subscribe CTAs) and aspect ratios (square 1:1, vertical 9:16, horizontal 16:9). The soft pink/blush (#E8DCD4) surface is used for SMM event graphics as an alternative to the standard cream.

primary.usage: Core brand identifier. Coral-orange used as dominant background blocks, hero sections, primary CTA buttons, accent bars. Appears in every HOSQ material at 12–24% coverage. Use for high-impact areas that need to feel warm, energetic, and distinctly HOSQ.

secondary.usage: Supporting brand color. Lavender-violet used as equal-weight background blocks alongside primary, gradient endpoints, large surface fills. Appears at 10–24% coverage in materials. Use for secondary surfaces, hover states, and to create the signature coral-violet split composition.

accent.usage: Emerald green used in organic visual shapes, tertiary background elements, and as a contrast accent against coral and violet. Appears at 8–15% in materials. Use for badges, tags, success states, and visual punctuation.

accent_blue.usage: Periwinkle-teal blue appearing in abstract visual compositions and as part of the organic gradient system. Use for links, informational elements, and as a cool counterpoint to the warm primary.

accent_pink.usage: Hot pink/magenta appearing in background gradients and organic shapes. Use for decorative gradients, highlight effects, and to extend the psychedelic visual palette.

readable_mid.note: NEW: accessible replacement for mid gray. Use for secondary text, captions, metadata on cream/white surfaces. Passes WCAG AA 4.6:1 on #EBE2D6.

mid.note: DECORATIVE ONLY. Fails AA on cream (2.7:1). Use only for borders, dividers, placeholder ghost text, disabled states — never for readable text.

light.note: DECORATIVE ONLY. Borders, dividers, subtle separators.

neutrals.note: Neutral ramp is warm-tinted, not pure gray. The lightest neutral (#EBE2D6) is the signature warm cream surface. Mid (#9A9596) and light (#C5C4C3) are DECORATIVE — never use for readable text. Use readable_mid (#6B6566) for secondary text instead.

contrast_pairs.note: Pragmatic approach: AA 4.5:1 for normal text, AA Large 3:1 for bold 18px+ or regular 24px+. Decorative mid (#9A9596) is restricted to non-text uses.

pairs.name: primary_text_on_surface
pairs.usage: Default body text, headings, all readable content on cream surface

pairs.name: primary_text_on_white
pairs.usage: Text on white elevated cards, overlays, clean SMM variants

pairs.name: primary_text_on_blush
pairs.usage: Text on soft blush SMM backgrounds

pairs.name: secondary_text_on_surface
pairs.usage: Captions, metadata, secondary info, footer text on cream

pairs.name: secondary_text_on_white
pairs.usage: Captions and secondary text on white cards

pairs.name: dark_text_on_coral
pairs.usage: Body text on coral backgrounds, paragraphs on hero sections

pairs.name: white_on_coral_large
pairs.usage: BUTTONS, pill tags, h1-h3 headings on coral. Bold 18px+ or regular 24px+ ONLY. Brand signature.

pairs.name: white_on_green_large
pairs.usage: Tags, badges, pill labels on green accent. Bold 18px+ ONLY.

pairs.name: dark_text_on_green
pairs.usage: Body text on green accent backgrounds

pairs.name: readable_mid_on_dark
pairs.usage: Secondary text on dark backgrounds. Large text only.

forbidden_pairs.reason: Mid gray on cream fails all WCAG levels. Use #6B6566 instead.
Light gray on cream — invisible. Decorative only.
White on coral fails AA normal. Only use for 18px+ bold or 24px+ regular.
White on green fails AA normal. Only use for 18px+ bold or 24px+ regular.

semantic.note: Semantic colors derived from brand palette. Success reuses accent green; error reuses primary coral; info reuses accent blue. Warning uses a desaturated peach from the gradient system.

backgrounds.note: HOSQ uses warm cream (#EBE2D6) as the default content surface — NOT pure white. Pure white is reserved for elevated cards, overlays, and clean SMM template variants. Soft blush pink (#E8DCD4) is used for SMM event graphics with visual overlays. Hero/promotional backgrounds use full-bleed coral or violet fields, or the signature organic gradient compositions.

brand_primary.usage: Primary brand gradient. Coral to violet diagonal. Use for hero sections, feature highlights, and immersive backgrounds.

brand_full.usage: Extended brand gradient incorporating all three core colors. Use for wide backgrounds and panoramic compositions.

warm_surface.usage: Subtle warm surface gradient for content areas. Almost imperceptible warmth shift.

button_pill.note: HOSQ uses fully-rounded pill-shaped buttons and tags. Seen in slides as 'Public good', 'Freemium', 'We are here' labels. Primary buttons use coral with white text. Secondary/outline buttons use cream or transparent with dark text.

type_scale.description: Custom scale optimized for HOSQ's airy aesthetic. Big jumps between levels ensure each is visually distinct. Scale: 72→48→36→24→20→18→16→14→12.

scale.family: editorial: Austin Cy / product: Cera Pro
scale.usage: Website hero headlines (editorial context). Largest text on any page.
Page/section title. Primary heading on web pages, top of content hierarchy.
Feature headings, major section dividers, card group titles.
Card titles, subsection headers, sidebar headings.
Small headings, list group titles, form section labels.
Lead paragraphs, intro text, featured descriptions. Slightly larger than default body.
Default body text. Paragraphs, descriptions, content.
UI text, form labels, helper text, secondary info.

slide_override.note: Presentation slides use Cera Pro Medium 62pt for slide titles (per original template spec). This is a context-specific override, not part of the web type scale.

display.usage: EDITORIAL CONTEXT ONLY. Hero headlines on landing pages, poster titles. Austin Cy is a sharp transitional serif with high contrast strokes — gives HOSQ its editorial, art-world feel. Never use in product contexts (SMM, UI, apps).
display.style_notes: Austin Cy-Bold has elegant, high-contrast letterforms with sharp serifs. It reads as editorial, sophisticated, and art-forward.

h1.usage: Page/section title. Primary heading on web pages. In product contexts this is the largest heading.

h2.usage: Feature headings, major section dividers, card group titles.

h3.usage: Card titles, subsection headers, sidebar headings.

h4.usage: Small headings, list group titles, form section labels. Medium weight for subtle distinction.

body_lg.usage: Lead paragraphs, intro text, featured descriptions. Slightly larger body for emphasis.

body.usage: Default body text. Paragraphs, descriptions, slide body text, document content.

small.usage: UI text, form labels, helper text, secondary info.

caption.usage: Footnotes, metadata, labels, timestamps, photo credits.

category_label.usage: Category/type labels on SMM posts and slides: 'LECTURE', 'PARTNER', 'CONCERT', etc. Always uppercase, bold, with wide tracking. Positioned top-left corner of the canvas. This is a structural element that anchors the post type.

smm_title.usage: Main event title on social media posts. Very large, bold Cera Pro. Dominates the upper portion of the canvas. Line breaks are intentional and controlled for visual rhythm.

smm_speaker.usage: Speaker name, subtitle, or secondary info on SMM posts. Regular weight to contrast with the bold title above.

smm_date.usage: Date line on event posts. Bold weight for emphasis. Time appears on the line below in regular weight.

smm_body.usage: Bio text, descriptions, and long-form content on SMM cards. Regular weight with strategic bold spans for names and key terms (Cera Pro Bold inline). Large body size for social media readability.

smm_quote.usage: Pull quotes on SMM cards. Bold weight, large size, fills most of the card. Used for speaker quotes and mission statements.

smm_footer.usage: Venue name (bottom-left) and URL 'hosq.co' (bottom-right) on every SMM post. Small, understated, consistent across all templates.

logo_wordmark.usage: The 'hosq' wordmark in the logo is set in Cera Pro Bold, always lowercase. The logo system consists of the diamond prism icon above a horizontal rule, with the wordmark below.
logo_wordmark.note: Logo must always appear as a complete unit (icon + wordmark). Never separate the components. Available in black (#231F20) and light/white variants.

font_scope.description: Two context modes determine font selection. AI must classify the task first, then apply the correct font system.

editorial.description: Website hero sections, landing pages, posters, email covers, print materials, festival graphics, investor decks, long-form editorial articles
editorial.display_usage: h1 hero headlines ONLY. Never for h2-h6. Never for body text. Never below 36px.

product.description: SMM posts, presentations, UI/UX, dashboards, apps, forms, documentation, newsletters, all digital product interfaces
product.display_usage: All headings including h1. No Austin Cy.
product.heading_font: Cera Pro Bold / Cera Pro Medium
product.heading_usage: Cera Pro Medium 62pt for slide titles (per template spec). Cera Pro Bold for SMM titles, UI headings.

classification_hints.if: poster, flyer, print ad, festival graphic
blog post hero, editorial article header
Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, social media post
app UI, dashboard, form, settings page

font_scope.ambiguous_cases: When unsure, default to product (Cera Pro only). It's safer to omit Austin Cy than to misuse it.

typography.font_pairing_rationale: Austin Cy (editorial serif) + Cera Pro (geometric sans) creates a deliberate tension between art-world sophistication and tech-forward clarity. Austin Cy signals cultural depth; Cera Pro signals modern utility. This pairing reflects HOSQ's positioning at the intersection of creative culture and AI technology. Austin Cy is RESERVED for editorial contexts (websites, posters, print) — never for product contexts (SMM, UI, apps, presentations).
typography.web_fallback_strategy: When Austin Cy is unavailable (web contexts without custom font loading), substitute with 'Playfair Display' (Google Fonts) for display text — it shares the high-contrast transitional serif character. When Cera Pro is unavailable, substitute with 'DM Sans' (Google Fonts) — closest match for geometric sans with similar x-height and rounded forms. Second fallback: 'Inter'.

spacing.note: Spacing inferred from presentation slide layouts. HOSQ uses generous whitespace — content slides have substantial margins (~48px equivalent at 1920x1080) with clean separation between elements. The overall spacing rhythm feels airy and unhurried.

composition_patterns.split_background: Vertical 50/50 split with coral (#FF6F55) on one half and violet (#C47CF1) on the other. Organic abstract shapes overlap the divide. Logo centered at the intersection.
composition_patterns.content_slide: Warm cream (#EBE2D6) full-bleed background. Subtle gradient overlay at edges (peach/green tints). Black text. Logo bottom-right corner. Content left-aligned with generous margins.
composition_patterns.hero_immersive: Full-bleed abstract visual background from the brand's organic shape library. Logo and text overlaid in black. Grain/noise texture throughout.
composition_patterns.card_layout: White or cream cards on cream surface. No visible shadows — separation through subtle color contrast only. Pill-shaped tags/badges for categorization.
composition_patterns.timeline_flowchart: Clean line-based diagrams on cream background. Rounded rectangular nodes with thin borders. Connecting lines with subtle curves.
composition_patterns.smm_event_visual: Soft blush (#E8DCD4) background with organic grain visual positioned center-right. Category label top-left, title large bold below, speaker name regular weight, date bold, logo bottom-center, venue bottom-left, URL bottom-right.
composition_patterns.smm_text_card: Warm cream (#EBE2D6) background. Category label top-left. Body text fills center area with strategic bold spans. Logo bottom-center, venue bottom-left, URL bottom-right.
composition_patterns.smm_portrait: Full-bleed warm-toned portrait photo. Name in uppercase bold top-left. Role bottom-left. URL bottom-right. Minimal text overlay.
composition_patterns.smm_horizontal_split: Landscape format split: left ~55% has blush background with content and grain visual; right ~45% is pure white. Used for Facebook/Twitter covers and wide banners.
composition_patterns.smm_subscribe_cta: Split composition: white content area with large 'subscribe & join' title, body text, and category tags. Adjacent area has full-bleed brand visual (violet gradient + organic shapes).

visual_texture.grain: HOSQ brand visuals use heavy grain/noise textures on organic shapes, creating a tactile, screen-printed aesthetic. This is a key brand differentiator.
visual_texture.transparency: Layered shapes use partial transparency, creating rich color mixing where elements overlap.
visual_texture.organic_shapes: Abstract biomorphic forms — spheres, blobs, radiating shapes — rendered with grain. These are NOT geometric or precise; they feel natural and hand-crafted.

logo_usage.components: Diamond prism icon + horizontal rule + 'hosq' wordmark (lowercase, Cera Pro Bold)
logo_usage.placement: Bottom-right corner on content slides. Centered on promotional materials and covers.
logo_usage.safe_space: Maintain clear space equal to the height of the 'h' letterform on all sides.
logo_usage.never: Never colorize the logo with brand colors. Never separate icon from wordmark. Never use on busy backgrounds without sufficient contrast.

components.description: Complete component specifications for AI to build any HOSQ interface. Each component includes background, border, radius, padding, and key styling rules.

nav.note: Clean, minimal nav. No shadows, no borders. Logo left, links center or right, CTA pill far right.

hero.title_font: Austin Cy Bold (editorial) or Cera Pro Bold (product)
hero.cta_buttons: 1-2 pill buttons. Primary: dark bg (#231F20) + cream text on gradient heroes, or coral bg + white text on neutral heroes. Secondary: outline with dark border.
hero.decorative_blob: Optional blurred organic shape (accent green or blue, opacity 0.2-0.3, blur 40px) positioned top-right or bottom-left for depth.
hero.note: Heroes always feel immersive and generous. Never cramped. Text left-aligned, max-width constrained.

section.note: Sections alternate between white and cream backgrounds. Never two white or two cream in a row. Generous vertical padding (96px) creates the airy feel.

card.hover: none by default. Optional: subtle translateY(-2px) + transition 200ms
card.note: Cards get their contrast from background color difference with parent section — NOT from shadows or borders. On white section: cream cards. On cream section: white cards.

input.note: Clean, minimal inputs. No inner shadows. Focus = border darkens to near-black. Placeholder uses decorative mid gray.

select.note: Selects share input styling. Dropdown is the ONE exception where a subtle shadow is allowed — it needs to float above content.

textarea.note: Same as input but taller. Vertical resize only.

primary.note: White text on coral. Brand signature. Ensure text is bold 18px+ for AA Large compliance.

footer.layout: Logo left or center top. Links row. Copyright + location bottom.
footer.note: Dark inverse footer. Cream text. Secondary info in decorative mid gray (OK here because it's on dark bg at 4.2:1 large text).

modal.note: Modals are the exception where shadows are used — they must visually float above the overlay.

toast.note: Dark pill-shaped notifications. Accent color as left border or icon tint.

avatar.note: Circular avatars. Fallback shows initials on violet background.

divider.note: Thin, subtle. Uses decorative light gray. On dark backgrounds use dark neutral.

table.note: Warm-tinted table. Cream header, alternating white/warm rows. Rounded outer container.

anti_patterns.description: What HOSQ is NOT. AI should check generated output against these and correct any matches.
visual: No Material Design shadows or elevation system
No tight, dense, information-heavy layouts (no dashboards-as-default)
No rounded-corner device mockups or screenshot frames
No stock-photo-style imagery (smiling people at desks, handshakes)
No sharp geometric patterns (hexagons, triangles, grid overlays) as decoration
No neon/fluorescent colors outside the brand palette
No glassmorphism, neumorphism, or frosted-glass effects
typographic: No monospace fonts in UI (code blocks excepted)
No display/decorative fonts besides Austin Cy
No all-caps body text (only category labels are uppercase)
tonal: No aggressive sales language ('limited time!', 'act now!', 'don't miss out!')
No exclamation marks in headlines or CTAs

brands_hosq_is_NOT_like.reason: Too minimal, too cold, too developer-focused
Too utilitarian, too tool-like, lacks art-world warmth
Too playful, too colorful, too consumer-casual
Too energetic, too fast, too youth-focused

brands_hosq_IS_like.reason: Clean typography, warm palette, intellectual tone
Cultural institution with modern digital presence
Art-forward, experimental visuals with professional rigor

brand_voice.description: Tone and copy guidelines for AI-generated text that accompanies HOSQ design outputs.

writing_rules.case: Prefer lowercase for display text and CTAs ('subscribe & join', 'get started'). Sentence case for body. UPPERCASE only for category labels (LECTURE, PARTNER, CONCERT).
writing_rules.punctuation: Periods for complete sentences. No exclamation marks. Em-dashes (—) preferred over semicolons for elegant pauses. Use the en-dash (–) for ranges.
writing_rules.length: Headlines: 2-6 words. Subheads: 5-12 words. Body: short paragraphs (2-3 sentences max). Let the design breathe.
writing_rules.vocabulary: Use art-world and cultural vocabulary naturally: 'curator', 'practice', 'discourse', 'residency', 'commissioning', 'programme'. Avoid tech jargon unless describing HOSQ-AI specifically.
writing_rules.cta_style: Soft, invitational CTAs. 'get started', 'learn more', 'subscribe & join', 'explore'. Never 'Buy now!', 'Sign up FREE!', 'Don't miss out!'.

voice_examples.bad_headline: Revolutionize Your Grant Writing with AI!
voice_examples.good_body: HOSQ-AI helps creators think through and refine cultural projects into structured, investment-ready proposals.
voice_examples.bad_body: Our amazing AI tool will supercharge your grant applications and help you win more funding than ever before!

constraints.description: Machine-readable do/don't rules. AI MUST check every output against this list. Rules are ordered by severity.

rules.description: Logo must be #231F20 (dark) or #DDD5CA (light) ONLY. Never apply brand colors (coral, violet, green) to the logo.
Logo is always a complete unit: diamond prism icon + horizontal rule + 'hosq' wordmark. Never use icon alone. Never use wordmark alone.
The wordmark is always 'hosq' in lowercase. Never 'HOSQ', 'Hosq', or other capitalizations in the logo itself. (Brand name in text can be uppercase.)
Primary page/section background must be #EBE2D6 (warm cream), NOT #FFFFFF. White is only for elevated cards, overlays, and alternating sections.
All neutrals must be warm-tinted. Never use blue-gray, cool-gray, or pure gray (#808080, #666, #999). Use the warm neutral ramp: #231F20 → #4A4545 → #6B6566 → #9A9596 → #C5C4C3 → #EBE2D6.
Never use #9A9596 or #C5C4C3 for readable text on cream/white backgrounds. These are DECORATIVE ONLY (borders, dividers, disabled states). Use #6B6566 for secondary readable text.
White (#FFFFFF) text on coral (#FF6F55) is allowed ONLY for bold text 18px+ or regular text 24px+. Below these sizes use #231F20 on coral instead.
White (#FFFFFF) text on green (#14B672) is allowed ONLY for bold text 18px+ or regular text 24px+. Below these sizes use #231F20 on green instead.
Austin Cy is EDITORIAL ONLY (landing pages, posters, print). Never use Austin Cy in SMM posts, presentations, UI, apps, forms, dashboards, or documentation. Use Cera Pro Bold instead.
Austin Cy is ONLY for display h1 headlines at 36px+. Never use for h2-h6, body text, captions, labels, or any text below 36px.
Body text is always Cera Pro (sans-serif). Never use Austin Cy, Georgia, Times, or any serif font for paragraphs, descriptions, or running text.

No drop shadows on static UI elements (cards, containers, sections, nav). Separation is achieved through background color contrast ONLY. Exceptions: hover state shadows (temporary), modals (must float), select dropdowns (must float), toasts (must float).
Cards never have visible borders. Use background color contrast with parent section. On white section: cream card. On cream section: white card.
Page sections MUST alternate between #FFFFFF and #EBE2D6 backgrounds. Never two white or two cream sections in a row.
HOSQ uses airy visual density. Section padding minimum 96px vertical. Card padding minimum 24px. Never create dense, cramped layouts. When in doubt, add more whitespace.
All buttons, tags, and badges use fully-rounded pill shape (border-radius: 999px). Never use rectangular or slightly-rounded buttons.

Brand visual elements use organic biomorphic forms (spheres, blobs, radiating shapes) with grain texture. Never use sharp geometric shapes (triangles, hexagons, grid patterns) for decorative elements.
Text is always solid color. Never apply gradient fills, rainbow effects, or color-shifting to text.
Brand visual backgrounds should include grain/noise texture. This is a key brand differentiator. Apply via SVG filters or CSS noise overlay.
HOSQ is an art-forward cultural brand. No emoji in UI, headings, buttons, or labels. Emoji may appear only in user-generated content.
HOSQ tone is intellectual and unhurried. Avoid exclamation marks in UI text, headlines, and CTAs. Use periods or no punctuation.
HOSQ prefers lowercase for display text and CTAs ('subscribe & join' not 'Subscribe & Join'). Category labels are the exception (always UPPERCASE). Sentence case for body text.
Text is predominantly left-aligned. Center alignment only for: logo placement, partner logos, and short single-line CTAs. Never center-align paragraphs or multi-line headings.
Do not introduce colors outside the defined palette. No random blues, yellows, oranges, or reds. All colors must come from the brand spec.
All SMM posts MUST follow the standard grid: category label top-left, logo bottom-center, venue bottom-left, URL bottom-right. This is non-negotiable.

motion.description: 300ms ease-out transitions. Unhurried, confident motion that reflects HOSQ's airy brand character. Not fast, not slow — deliberate.

timing.usage_note: Default (300ms) for buttons, cards, color changes. Fast (150ms) for active/pressed states. Slow (500ms) for scroll-triggered entrance animations.

card.note: Shadow on hover is the ONE exception to 'no shadows' rule — it's temporary and provides interaction feedback.

focus_states.note: Dark near-black focus ring, 2px offset. Visible on all backgrounds. Never use browser default blue.

scroll_animations.trigger: element enters viewport at 80% scroll position
scroll_animations.note: Subtle fade-in-up for sections and cards as they scroll into view. Small distance (24px) — never dramatic. Stagger children by 100ms for sequential reveal.

page_transitions.note: Simple opacity fade between pages. No slide or scale effects.

reduced_motion.fallback: Disable all transforms and scroll animations. Keep color transitions only at 150ms. Respect user accessibility preferences.
reduced_motion.note: Always wrap motion in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference). Users who need reduced motion should get instant state changes with no transforms.

smm_templates.source: hosq LECTURE TEMPLATES 2025.pdf, Post Cover 2026.png, hosq insta labs/lectors/curators/partners/concert TEMPLATE.ai files

square.label: Instagram Feed / General
square.usage: Primary format for most post types

horizontal.label: Facebook / Twitter / Landscape
horizontal.usage: Wide cover format, often uses split composition (content left, white/visual right)

layout_grid.description: All SMM templates share a consistent spatial grid anchored by fixed zones.

event_cover.description: Primary event announcement post. Most common template type.

speaker_bio.description: Speaker biography / description card. Text-heavy with inline bold for names and key terms.

typography_hierarchy.example: **Maria Cristina Didero** is an Italian curator, author, and design scholar...

speaker_bio.note: Names and key terms are set in Cera Pro Bold inline within Regular body text. This creates emphasis without disrupting the reading flow.

speaker_quote.description: Pull quote or statement card from a speaker. Large bold text, minimal elements.

typography_hierarchy.example: A lecture in Yerevan to connect with the Armenian international design scene...

speaker_portrait.description: Full-bleed portrait photo with minimal text overlay.
speaker_portrait.photo_treatment: Portraits use a warm, desaturated tone that harmonizes with the cream/blush brand palette. Soft, editorial lighting. Neutral or warm-toned backgrounds.

partner_logo.description: Partner organization showcase. Single centered logo with partner label.

typography_hierarchy.example: The State Philharmonia of Armenia, Yerevan

subscribe_cta.description: Subscribe / follow / join call-to-action card. Split composition with white content area and vibrant visual.

background_variants.name: split_white_visual
background_variants.description: Left half white, right half full-bleed brand visual (violet + grain organic shapes)

typography_hierarchy.example: hosq is an international cultural organization fostering the creative industry and honoring talents.

ghost_variant.description: Every template has a 'ghost' variant where all text is rendered in #DDD5CA on white background. This serves as a visual template/placeholder showing the layout structure without content. Used internally for design handoff.

horizontal_split_pattern.description: Horizontal (landscape) templates use a distinctive split: content occupies the left ~55% on a blush/pink background with grain visual overlay; the right ~45% is pure white. This creates a strong visual anchor while maintaining breathing room.

smm_templates.note: The SMM system is highly modular. Each content type follows the same spatial grid (category top-left, logo bottom-center, venue bottom-left, URL bottom-right) while varying the central content area. This makes the system extremely predictable for AI generation — any new content type can be created by filling the established zones.

responsive.description: Full responsive system with 4 breakpoints, fluid type using clamp(), and adaptive spacing. Mobile-first approach.

fluid_type_scale.description: Type scale values at each breakpoint. Use CSS clamp() for fluid interpolation between mobile and desktop.

fluid_spacing.description: Key spacing values at each breakpoint. Section padding and margins scale down on mobile.

prompt_fragments.description: Ready-to-use prompt snippets that AI can inject into generation tasks to ensure on-brand output.
prompt_fragments.landing_page: Build a HOSQ landing page. Use warm cream (#EBE2D6) as the default page background — never pure white. Hero section uses the brand gradient (coral #FF6F55 → violet #C47CF1 at 135°) with an optional blurred organic shape. Hero headline in Austin Cy Bold at clamp(40px, 5vw+16px, 72px), all other text in Cera Pro. Body text #231F20, secondary text #6B6566. Pill-shaped buttons (border-radius: 999px). Sections alternate white/cream backgrounds. No drop shadows on cards — contrast through background color only. Cards on white sections are cream; cards on cream sections are white. 12px border-radius on cards, 8px on inputs. Transitions at 300ms ease-out. Mobile-first with 4 breakpoints. Tone: intellectual, unhurried, lowercase CTAs.
prompt_fragments.smm_post: Create a HOSQ social media post. Use the SMM grid: category label (uppercase, bold, 0.12em tracking) top-left. Main title in Cera Pro Bold, very large. Speaker/subtitle in Cera Pro Regular. Logo (hosq diamond + wordmark) bottom-center. Venue bottom-left. URL 'hosq.co' bottom-right. Background: warm cream #EBE2D6 for text cards, soft blush #E8DCD4 for visual cards with grain overlay. All text #231F20. NO Austin Cy in SMM — only Cera Pro. Square: 1080×1080. Vertical: 1080×1920. Horizontal: 1456×816.
prompt_fragments.website_section: Create a HOSQ website section. Background alternates: if previous section was white, use cream #EBE2D6 (and vice versa). Section padding: clamp(48px, 8vw, 96px) vertical. Section title in Cera Pro Bold 36px (h2). Content in 12-column grid, max-width 1200px. Cards use 12px radius, 24px padding, no shadows, no borders. Tags are pill-shaped (11px, bold, 999px radius). Link hover: color shifts to coral #FF6F55 with animated underline.
prompt_fragments.presentation_slide: Create a HOSQ presentation slide. Background: warm cream #EBE2D6. Title in Cera Pro Medium 62pt. Body in Cera Pro Regular. All text #231F20. Logo in bottom-right corner in black (#231F20). NO Austin Cy — presentations are product context, Cera Pro only. Generous margins (48px all sides). Use pill-shaped tags for categories.
prompt_fragments.email_header: Create a HOSQ email header. This is editorial context — headline can use Austin Cy Bold at 36px+. Body in Cera Pro Regular. Background options: brand gradient (coral→violet), solid coral, or warm cream. Text always #231F20. Logo centered or left. CTA pill button. Warm, intellectual tone.
prompt_fragments.poster: Create a HOSQ poster. Editorial context — display headline in Austin Cy Bold 72px+. Organic biomorphic shapes with heavy grain/noise texture as background visual. Color palette: coral #FF6F55, violet #C47CF1, emerald #14B672. Text #231F20. Logo (complete unit: diamond + wordmark) in black or light beige #DDD5CA. HOSQ event posters use the signature split composition: 50/50 coral/violet with organic shapes overlapping the divide.
